props.message:
AT THE GATEWAY

"Walk gently.
These were saved for you."

Brass dedication on a bench:

"For those who sat here
and for those who will."

Brass dedication on a bench:

"In memory of Pell, who would not let the fountain be removed. The fountain is still here."

Brass dedication on a bench:

"For all who came back tired.
The hedges keep your shape."

A long inscription beside the central garden:

"Lirien sets her care on what was, even when no one asked her to keep it. The Yard is one such place. The bench where you are sitting is another."

Inscription on the dry fountain:

"It ran for a thousand years.
It is resting.
Walk well."

props.open_message: A heart-flower from the Yard. It smells faintly of cedar.
A fairy bottle, kept under the dedication-bench. The Yard is generous.

root.speaker: Groundskeeper Tess
root.text: Welcome to the Yard, traveler. Wipe your feet on nothing in particular — the path keeps itself. What can I tell you?

tend.speaker: Groundskeeper Tess
tend.text: I do, mostly. The leaves help. The hedges have been here longer than my line. Sometimes I think they tend me.

here.speaker: Groundskeeper Tess
here.text: Old gardens. Older benches. A fountain that has not run in three winters but has not stopped being a fountain. We keep what was, even when no one asks.

lirien.speaker: Groundskeeper Tess
lirien.text: She has walked here. I have not seen her, but the leaves know. They turn a little when the wind is from a direction the wind is not from. It is a kind of unease, and a kind of welcome.

bye.speaker: Groundskeeper Tess
bye.text: Walk gently. The Yard remembers walking.

root.speaker: Poet Nira
root.text: Hush — I'm almost there. Just a couplet, that's all I came for. Would you hear it?

couplet.speaker: Poet Nira
couplet.text:
"What was kept here was kept by no one's asking;
the leaves remembered, and the leaves were enough."

thanks.speaker: Poet Nira
thanks.text: It came easy. They mostly don't. Walk well, traveler. Tell the leaves I said thank you.

bye.speaker: Poet Nira
bye.text: Walk well. The couplet will keep.

root.speaker: Young Wyrdkin Jen
root.text: Oh — hello. Sorry. I was thinking. You're not interrupting, I just — I'm thinking. Did you need something?

ask1.speaker: Young Wyrdkin Jen
ask1.text: Oh — nothing, really. I came here to think and I'd rather not say what about. Sorry. The Yard is good for not-saying.

ask2.speaker: Young Wyrdkin Jen
ask2.text: ...all right. My brother went up the Stoneroost trail at the start of the spring and hasn't come back yet. He said he might not. I'm trying to decide if I'm allowed to be angry, or only sad. The hedges are helping me work it out.

thanks.speaker: Young Wyrdkin Jen
thanks.text: Thank you. Walk well. Tell anyone climbing toward Stoneroost that Jen's brother owes her a letter.

bye.speaker: Young Wyrdkin Jen
bye.text: Walk well. Thank you for being kind.

props.open_message: Inside the basin of the long-dry fountain, half-buried under wind-blown leaves. A piece of heart. The Yard saved it for whoever sat longest.
